narrativeOwner: Prof. Hao Li, Dean of UG Division & Prof. Dezhen Song, VP Graduate Education

assessment.headline: Graduate students are gaining external recognition at top venues and from leading institutions. An MSc student received the Google DeepMind Scholarship — a first for MBZUAI — and a PhD student was selected for the KAUST Visiting Researcher program. Meanwhile, graduate student policies around funding and faculty matching are being formalized to address equity and clarity concerns.
whereWeStand: MSc student Yara Mahmoud became the first MBZUAI student to receive the Google DeepMind Scholarship, one of 20 globally. Validates graduate program quality at the highest level.
PhD student Chen Wei was selected for the KAUST Visiting Researcher program on protein structure prediction, strengthening the cross-institutional research relationship.
MSc student Arjun Mehta received the Best Paper Award at the AAAI 2026 Student Track for work on Arabic language model attention mechanisms — directly relevant to JAIS.
18 students currently on the Dean's List, up from 15 last semester.
whatsNext: Graduate student funding policies are being formalized — starting with the 2025 cohort, second-year master's students become the financial responsibility of their matched faculty advisor. **Deadline: March 31 · Owner: Provost Office**
The Graduate Student Handbook is being comprehensively updated to reflect current policies and funding structures. **Deadline: Before fall 2026 · Owner: VP Graduate Education**
A new research support fund is being piloted for graduate students presenting at Tier 1 venues — covering registration, travel, and poster costs. **Owner: VP Graduate Education**

graduate.achievementDataNote: Student highlight data is currently based on available sources. Comprehensive data requested from Faculty Affairs.

assessment.headline: The undergraduate program is producing early results that exceed expectations for a second-year program. Three students won prizes at the Regional AI Hackathon, and an undergraduate paper was accepted at ACL 2026 — a first for the program. The curriculum is being actively refined for Cohort 2, and a bridging program for UAE nationals is in design.
whereWeStand: Three UG students won prizes at the Regional AI Hackathon in Dubai, including first place (Omar Al-Farsi) and Best Social Impact Award (Aisha Khalid).
Ravi Sundaram's paper on Arabic NLP was accepted at ACL 2026 — the first major venue publication from the UG program and a validation of the Rector's Research Program.
Fatima Al-Blooshi completed a rector's research project on AI for heritage preservation that drew pilot interest from the Abu Dhabi Department of Culture and Tourism.
whatsNext: Cohort 2 curriculum is being refined with expanded focus on life sciences, physics, and business alongside AI. Longer class durations with active learning being evaluated for fall 2026. **Deadline: Spring 2026 · Owner: Dean of UG Division**

A 12-month bridging program for UAE nationals lacking STEM preparation is in design — non-residential, fee-paying, with online self-paced options being explored. **Target: Cohort 3 · Owner: Dean of UG Division**
A student persona and culture profile initiative is underway to define what makes the MBZUAI undergraduate experience distinctive. **Deadline: Spring 2026 · Owner: Dean of UG Division**
Institutional support for student competitions is being formalized — identifying worthy competitions, matching faculty coaches, and securing budget for attendance. **Owner: Dean of UG Division**
Rector's Research Program expanding for Cohort 2 — matching students with faculty mentors and providing structured research milestones. **Deadline: Fall 2026 · Owner: Dean of UG Division**

achievements.description: Led a team of three MBZUAI undergraduates to first place at the Regional AI Hackathon hosted by DIFC. The team built a real-time Arabic sentiment analysis tool for financial news using a fine-tuned JAIS model. 48 teams from 12 universities competed.
achievements.associatedAward: AED 50,000 prize + DIFC Innovation Lab incubation offer
achievements.significance: First MBZUAI team to win a regional hackathon. Tool built on JAIS model — strong narrative for MarComms.

achievements.achievementHeadline: Best Social Impact Award — Regional AI Hackathon, Dubai
achievements.description: Developed an AI-powered accessibility tool for Arabic-speaking visually impaired users, combining computer vision and Arabic TTS. Recognized for best social impact project.
achievements.associatedAward: Best Social Impact Award + AED 15,000 prize
achievements.significance: Emirati female student winning a social impact award — excellent for government and diversity reporting.

achievements.description: First-author paper on 'Cross-Dialectal Arabic Understanding via Adaptive Prompt Tuning' accepted at ACL 2026 main conference. This is the first major language-AI venue publication from an MBZUAI undergraduate student. The work was conducted as part of the Rector's Research Program.
achievements.associatedPublication: Cross-Dialectal Arabic Understanding via Adaptive Prompt Tuning — ACL 2026 (Main Conference)
achievements.significance: First undergraduate publication at a top language-AI venue. Validates the Rector's Research Program model. Directly relevant to JAIS and the Arabic language technology narrative.

achievements.description: Awarded the Google DeepMind Scholarship for graduate students in AI. One of 20 recipients globally. The scholarship includes full funding, a summer research placement at DeepMind London, and mentorship from a DeepMind researcher.
achievements.associatedAward: Google DeepMind Scholarship — full funding + summer placement
achievements.significance: First MBZUAI student to receive this scholarship. Strong brand signal — DeepMind selecting from MBZUAI validates graduate program quality.

achievements.description: Selected for KAUST's competitive Visiting Researcher program for a 3-month collaboration on protein structure prediction using diffusion models. One of 8 PhD students selected from universities across the MENA region.
achievements.significance: Strengthens MBZUAI-KAUST research relationship. Biological and Life Sciences cross-institutional collaboration.

achievements.achievementHeadline: Rector's Research Project — AI for Heritage Preservation
achievements.description: Completed a rector's research project developing a computer vision system for cataloguing and monitoring UAE cultural heritage artifacts. The system was demonstrated to the Department of Culture and Tourism Abu Dhabi, who expressed interest in a pilot deployment.
achievements.associatedAward: Invitation from DCT Abu Dhabi for pilot deployment
achievements.significance: Government-relevant application by an Emirati student. DCT pilot interest is a concrete institutional engagement opportunity.

achievements.achievementHeadline: Best Paper Award — AAAI 2026 Student Track
achievements.description: Received the Best Paper Award at the AAAI 2026 Student Abstract and Poster Program for work on 'Efficient Attention Mechanisms for Long-Context Arabic Language Models.' The work contributes directly to MBZUAI's JAIS model improvements.
achievements.associatedPublication: Efficient Attention Mechanisms for Long-Context Arabic Language Models — AAAI 2026 Student Track (Best Paper)
achievements.associatedAward: AAAI 2026 Student Track Best Paper Award
achievements.significance: Best Paper at a top-tier venue student track. JAIS relevance makes this valuable for board and MarComms narratives.

programDevelopments.title: Undergraduate Bridging Program
programDevelopments.owner: Prof. Hao Li, Dean of Undergraduate Division
programDevelopments.briefDescription: 12-month pre-undergraduate program to improve access for UAE nationals lacking STEM preparation.
programDevelopments.fullDescription: A new 12-month bridging program targeting local students, especially UAE nationals lacking STEM preparation. Offers math, coding, and AI foundational courses. Non-residential, fee-paying format with potential part-time, weekday, and weekend options. Online self-paced components being explored to scale and control labor costs. Design ensures competitive and non-guaranteed entry to the undergraduate program.
programDevelopments.currentProgress: Program structure and format under discussion. Key design decisions pending: residential vs. non-residential, fee structure, online vs. in-person balance. Concerns raised about creating an indefinite bridging loop without addressing root educational gaps.
programDevelopments.targetDate: Design completion targeted for mid-2026. Pilot launch TBD.

programDevelopments.title: UG Cohort 2 Curriculum Refinement
programDevelopments.owner: Prof. Hao Li, Dean of Undergraduate Division
programDevelopments.briefDescription: Curriculum update for Cohort 2 with expanded focus on life sciences, physics, and business alongside AI.
programDevelopments.fullDescription: Curriculum refinement underway with input from faculty, focusing on relevant, modern AI, life sciences, physics, and business content. Exploring longer class durations with active learning emphasis, starting potentially fall 2026. Based on peer benchmarking and student surveys from Cohort 1.
programDevelopments.currentProgress: Faculty input being collected. Peer benchmarking complete. Longer class format being evaluated for fall 2026. Faculty recruitment for UG prioritizing practical relevance over pure academic prestige.
programDevelopments.targetDate: Finalization by end of spring 2026 for fall 2026 implementation.

programDevelopments.title: Graduate Student Funding & Matching Policy
programDevelopments.briefDescription: New policies on student funding, faculty matching timelines, and performance expectations.
programDevelopments.fullDescription: Formulating clear policies on student funding, matching, and performance expectations. Starting with the 2025 cohort, second-year master's students become the financial responsibility of their matched faculty advisor. Addressing concerns about lack of formal exit policies, potential financial burdens from retaining underperforming students, and equity issues where junior faculty attract more students but have smaller packages.
programDevelopments.currentProgress: Provost Office drafting policy document. Consultation with division deans scheduled for mid-March. Key open questions: timing and criteria for matching, tiered funding for underperforming students, exit policy formalization.
programDevelopments.targetDate: Policy draft expected by end of March 2026.

programDevelopments.title: Student Persona & Culture Profile
programDevelopments.owner: Prof. Hao Li, Dean of Undergraduate Division
programDevelopments.briefDescription: Developing a detailed profile to differentiate the MBZUAI student experience from peer institutions.
programDevelopments.fullDescription: Initiative to define what makes the MBZUAI undergraduate student experience distinctive. Includes developing a detailed student persona, understanding student motivations and expectations, and designing a culture that differentiates MBZUAI from peer institutions. Linked to improved matchmaking between students and research advisors to enhance engagement.
programDevelopments.currentProgress: Initial student engagement surveys being designed. Research advisor matchmaking process being mapped. Benchmarking against peer undergraduate AI programs underway.
programDevelopments.targetDate: Initial profile expected by end of spring 2026.

programDevelopments.title: Graduate Student Handbook Update
programDevelopments.owner: Prof. Dezhen Song, VP Graduate Education
programDevelopments.briefDescription: Comprehensive update to the graduate student handbook covering policies, expectations, and resources.
programDevelopments.fullDescription: Full revision of the graduate student handbook to reflect current policies, updated funding structures, and clearer expectations for students and faculty advisors. Includes alignment with new funding policy changes and matching procedures.
programDevelopments.currentProgress: Revision underway. Key sections being rewritten to reflect 2025 cohort funding changes.
programDevelopments.targetDate: Updated handbook expected before fall 2026 semester.

programDevelopments.title: Commencement 2026
programDevelopments.briefDescription: Planning for the 2026 commencement ceremony — the university's largest graduating class to date.
programDevelopments.fullDescription: Commencement planning initiated for the Class of 2025 — MBZUAI's largest graduating cohort (estimated 130 graduates). Vendor identified for execution support. Provost Office requested deeper inclusion in planning and decision-making. MarComms leading logistics and production.
programDevelopments.currentProgress: Internal planning meetings commenced. Vendor engaged. Date and format under discussion.

programDevelopments.title: Institutional Competition Support Program
programDevelopments.owner: Prof. Hao Li, Dean of Undergraduate Division
programDevelopments.briefDescription: Formalizing support for students participating in external AI and STEM competitions.
programDevelopments.fullDescription: Developing a structured competition support pipeline: identifying worthy competitions aligned with MBZUAI strengths, matching faculty coaches to student teams, and establishing a dedicated budget for registration, travel, and materials. Aims to systematize the ad hoc support that produced the Regional AI Hackathon wins.
programDevelopments.currentProgress: Competition calendar for 2026-27 being compiled. Faculty coaching roster being developed. Budget proposal submitted to Provost Office.

programDevelopments.title: Rector's Research Program Expansion
programDevelopments.owner: Prof. Hao Li, Dean of Undergraduate Division
programDevelopments.briefDescription: Expanding the Rector's Research Program with structured faculty-student matching and research milestones.
programDevelopments.fullDescription: Building on the success of Cohort 1 (e.g., Ravi Sundaram's ACL publication, Fatima Al-Blooshi's heritage preservation project), the program is expanding for Cohort 2. Improvements include a formal faculty-student matching process, structured research milestones with mid-term review, and integration with the competition support pipeline for presentation opportunities.
programDevelopments.currentProgress: Faculty interest survey completed — 12 faculty members committed to mentoring UG researchers. Milestone framework being designed. Cohort 2 students will be invited to apply in fall 2026.
